metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how `_local_matches_prev` (or its replacement) translates local field name `'title'` to baseline key `'summary'` when reading from `get_baseline`, so the swap preserves the inbound-mirror for title/summary (not just description/priority/status/assignee).
findings.finding: The consumer swap mechanism has a silent field-name translation gap: `_local_matches_prev` is called with `field_name` values from `_INBOUND_MIRRORED_FIELDS` (which includes `'title'`), but `get_baseline` stores Jira-side field names from `_BASELINE_FIELDS` (which uses `'summary'`, not `'title'`). After the swap, `baseline.get('title')` will always return `None` (key absent), causing `_local_matches_prev` to fall through to local-wins for every title/summary field — silently breaking the inbound-mirror for title on every pass without any error signal.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section — Phase-3 consumer swap; `outbound_fields._local_matches_prev`; `_INBOUND_MIRRORED_FIELDS` ('title') vs `_BASELINE_FIELDS` ('summary')
findings.suggested_fix: In the swapped `_local_matches_prev`, add a `_LOCAL_TO_BASELINE_FIELD` mapping `{'title': 'summary'}` and use `baseline_fields.get(_LOCAL_TO_BASELINE_FIELD.get(field_name, field_name))` when looking up the baseline value, mirroring the same translation `_extract_jira_field` applies. The property test should explicitly cover the title→summary pair.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC: 'After flipping baseline_consumer_swap=true, at least 2 consecutive live passes (`rebar reconcile --mode live`) complete with zero divergences under the new flag; workflow run-ids recorded' — this makes the forward cutover path a verified completion gate.
findings.finding: Post-flip live exercise is absent: after baseline_consumer_swap is set to true the plan prescribes no live pass that exercises the swapped consumer path end-to-end; the >=10 shadow passes all run with the flag false, and the rollback dry-run only confirms zero mutations on the reverted path, so the new production code path is never validated by a live run before the session is declared complete.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL – Flip gate; AC line '[OPERATIONAL] Flip only after both gates; rollback rehearsed…'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Flip gate AC: 'Observe >=2 consecutive live passes (rebar reconcile --mode live, reconcile-bridge.yml) under baseline_consumer_swap=true with baseline_shadow_divergence==0 and no abort; record workflow run-ids as forward-flip evidence before the session is closed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit unit tests for the corrupt-store fail-closed detection path (e.g., `get_baseline` returns a non-dict value triggers abort) and the absent-baseline->None->local-wins path in the swapped consumer, distinct from the direction-preservation property test.
findings.finding: The new explicit corrupt-store detection logic introduced for `get_baseline` (the new branch that triggers fail-closed abort when the store returns a non-dict/corrupt entry) has no corresponding test specified anywhere in the plan. The only test called out is the direction-preservation property test, which is scoped to the five mirrored fields under the swapped consumer — not to corrupt-store abort behavior. This is new safety-critical conditional logic (a misconfiguration here silently falls through or aborts incorrectly) with no test.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — consumer swap section: 'add an explicit corrupt-store detection (get_baseline cannot itself signal "corrupt") so fail-closed is preserved'; AC line: 'corrupt-store fail-closed preserved (explicit detection)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add two unit test cases alongside the property test: (1) `get_baseline` returns a non-dict (e.g., a string or list) → assert the reconciler raises/aborts (fail-closed); (2) `get_baseline` returns None (absent entry) → assert local-wins branch is taken, matching behavior of the original `prev_snapshot` path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a unit or integration test for the `classifier_legacy_divergence` alert emission: assert the event fires with correctly typed fields (`classifier_count:int`, `legacy_count:int`, `divergent_pairs:list[str]`, `pass_id:str`) on a known-divergent fixture, and assert it does NOT fire (or fires scoped) when per-binding legacy decisions are not extractable.
findings.finding: The classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting introduces new emission logic (`classifier_legacy_divergence` event with typed fields: `classifier_count`, `legacy_count`, `divergent_pairs`, `pass_id`) but no test is specified anywhere in the plan to verify the alert is emitted correctly, that the typed fields are populated as specified, or that the scoping/documentation path (when per-binding legacy decisions are not extractable) behaves correctly.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — '[CODE] classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting' section; AC line: '`classifier_legacy_divergence` emitted with the typed fields above (or scoped+documented where per-binding legacy decisions aren't extractable)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the property test description which field-name vocabulary the swapped `_local_matches_prev` consumer receives and how the `title`↔`summary` (local↔Jira) name translation is handled — confirm the test exercises at least the `title`/`summary` pair through the full `get_baseline` → `_local_matches_prev` path.
findings.finding: The property test instruction says 'covering the five mirrored fields as MAPPED (local title/description/priority/status/assignee <-> Jira summary/description/priority/status/assignee — reconcile the two field-name sets `_INBOUND_MIRRORED_FIELDS` and `baseline_shadow._MIRRORED_FIELDS`)' but the plan never defines what the reconciliation output is. `_INBOUND_MIRRORED_FIELDS` uses local names (`title`) while `_MIRRORED_FIELDS` uses Jira names (`summary`). The swapped consumer (`_local_matches_prev` calling `get_baseline`) operates on local field names against baseline values keyed by Jira names. The test must exercise this cross-vocabulary boundary, but the plan doesn't specify the canonical name set the test inputs should use or how the field-name mapping is applied in the swapped path.
findings.location: [CODE] RED-first direction-preservation property test section; AC line 'RED-first direction-preservation property test (swapped consumer, five mapped fields)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the [CODE] property-test bullet: 'The swapped consumer receives LOCAL field names; `get_baseline` returns Jira-keyed values, so the swap implementation must translate `title`→`summary` before the lookup. The property test must cover this cross-name path by driving `_local_matches_prev("title", local_val, baseline_from_get_baseline)` and asserting the `summary` key in the baseline is used.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a co-located comparison protocol to the `classifier_legacy_divergence` spec: define the mapping from `DecisionKind` values to legacy differ 'actions' (or the extractable subset), and state the exact condition under which a pair is counted as divergent.
findings.finding: The `classifier_legacy_divergence` event schema is defined (`{classifier_count:int, legacy_count:int, divergent_pairs:list[str] of local_ids, pass_id:str}`) but the comparison protocol — what constitutes a 'divergent pair' between `classify()`'s `DecisionKind` output and the legacy differ's 'action' — is never specified and is not co-located with the schema. The closed `DecisionKind` enum has 8 values; the legacy differ's actions are a different vocabulary. The plan does not define the mapping between them, leaving the divergence-detection logic undefined.
findings.location: [CODE] classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting section; AC line 'classifier_legacy_divergence emitted with the typed fields above'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the exact corrupt-store detection signal in the CODE bullet, e.g. 'any non-dict return from get_baseline that is not None (e.g. a raw string, int, or list) triggers the corrupt-store fail-closed abort; schema-invalid dicts (missing required keys X, Y) also trigger abort.'
findings.finding: Corrupt-store detection mechanism is unspecified: the plan says 'add an explicit corrupt-store detection (get_baseline cannot itself signal corrupt)' but never defines what signal constitutes a corrupt store entry — type mismatch, schema validation failure, deserialization exception, unexpected key set, or something else — leaving a real design decision to the executor.
findings.location: Remaining CODE – Phase-3 consumer swap paragraph; AC line '[CODE] …corrupt-store fail-closed preserved (explicit detection)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the consumer-swap CODE bullet: 'A corrupt-store entry is defined as: get_baseline returns a non-None, non-dict value OR returns a dict missing the required keys [list them]. In either case, raise a CorruptStoreError and abort the pass (fail-closed). None maps to absent-baseline → local-wins.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the corrupt-store detection mechanism for the swapped consumer — e.g., expose a `BindingStore.baseline_state(local_id) -> Literal['absent','corrupt','ok']` helper, or define the inline inspection the consumer should perform, so the fail-closed abort path is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The plan requires 'add an explicit corrupt-store detection (get_baseline cannot itself signal "corrupt") so fail-closed is preserved' but does not specify the mechanism. Tool-verified: `get_baseline` returns `None` for BOTH absent (no `baseline` key) and corrupt (non-dict `baseline`) entries — both collapse to `None`. The plan acknowledges this but leaves the detection method open: inspect the raw binding entry before calling `get_baseline`? add a `has_corrupt_baseline()` method? Check `isinstance(entry.get('baseline'), dict)` inline? Without specifying the API surface, an implementor cannot write a correct fail-closed path.
findings.location: [CODE] Phase-3 consumer swap section; AC 'corrupt-store fail-closed preserved (explicit detection)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the [CODE] consumer-swap bullet: 'To distinguish corrupt from absent, call `entry = binding_store._data["bindings"].get(local_id)` then check `baseline = entry.get("baseline") if entry else None`; if `entry` exists and `baseline` is not a dict, treat as corrupt and raise/abort; if `entry` is absent or `baseline` is missing key, treat as absent → local-wins. Alternatively, expose a `BindingStore.baseline_state()` method.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit idempotency guarantee (or re-run guard) for the live heal: specify that the heal is safe to re-run after a partial failure, OR add a 'heal-in-progress' marker that prevents double-execution, covering GC-after-grace deletions specifically.
findings.finding: The live heal's idempotency under partial failure is not specified in this plan. The plan gates on backup failure (abort if backup fails) but does not state what happens if the heal operation itself partially completes and is re-run — particularly for GC-after-grace deletions (REB-530). A double-run of a non-idempotent deletion could silently corrupt binding-store or Jira state.
findings.location: [OPERATIONAL] Capped live heal / AC line: 'Capped live heal with a bilateral pre-heal backup'
findings.suggested_fix: Before the heal begins, write a `heal_run_<date>` marker to the binding store (or workflow artifact). On re-run, check the marker and skip any step already completed (checkpoint-style). For deletions, treat 'already absent' as a no-op success so the operation is naturally idempotent. Carry the idempotency guarantee forward from REB-530 explicitly in this plan's AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before the expand-contract drop step, enumerate all consumers of the 'binding_walk_census' event key (scripts, dashboards, log queries, canary checks) and confirm each is migrated to 'decision_census'.
findings.finding: The plan's census expand-contract task says 'migrate consumers to decision_census' but does not enumerate which external consumers currently read the 'binding_walk_census' event key. run_differs.py:535 is the emit site; any monitoring script, log parser, or downstream alert that reads 'binding_walk_census' is an unenumerated consumer that must be migrated before the key is dropped.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — Census key: expand-contract

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit idempotency/resumability guarantee for the capped live heal: specify that each of the 7 heal mutations (TERMINAL_TRANSITION, RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE, ADOPT) is a no-op if already applied (e.g., pre-check current Jira status before transitioning, check binding retirement state before GC), or document that bootstrap-strict MODE_CAPS=10 plus the circuit breaker bound any duplicate mutations to a safe maximum on re-runs.
findings.finding: The capped live heal of the 7 known drift instances has no stated idempotency or resumability guarantee. The plan mandates bilateral pre-heal backup and abort-if-backup-fails (covering rollback), but if the recurring 20-min reconcile-bridge workflow fires again before the fixed-point census assertion completes, or if the heal pass is interrupted mid-run, re-issuing archived→Done transitions, GC-after-grace deletions (f8b5-2f30 → REB-530), and adopt mutations on already-healed instances is not guaranteed safe. T11 requires a partially-completed migration to be resumable/idempotent.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Capped live heal under bootstrap-strict incl. the GC-after-grace deletion (f8b5-2f30 -> REB-530)'; AC line: '[OPERATIONAL] Capped live heal with a bilateral pre-heal backup (Jira JSON + tickets-branch tag); heal aborts if either backup fails; artifacts stored.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the required streak-evidence artifact: e.g. 'Attach a JSON array of the 10 qualifying baseline_shadow_check log events (pass_id, timestamp, divergent_count) as a named workflow artifact AND paste the 10 workflow run-IDs in the PR description.'
findings.finding: 'Evidence recorded' for the 10-pass streak is underspecified: the operational AC requires 'evidence recorded' but does not state what artifact constitutes evidence or where it must be stored, making in-session evaluability uncertain.
findings.location: AC line '[OPERATIONAL] >=10 consecutive clean shadow passes (log-derived streak, reset rule above) before the flip, NOT overlapping A3's live pass; evidence recorded'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'evidence recorded' to: 'Evidence: attach to the flip PR (a) a JSON array of the >=10 qualifying baseline_shadow_check events (fields: pass_id, timestamp, divergent==0) extracted from sync-log history, and (b) the corresponding GHA workflow run-IDs, confirming no >20-min gap and no A3 overlap.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific call site (file + function) where `decision_census` should be emitted, and confirm it covers all reconciler modes (dry-run, bootstrap-strict, bootstrap-throttle, live) — not just the binding-walk acting path.
findings.finding: The plan's census expand-contract step says 'emit BOTH `decision_census` and `binding_walk_census` for one release' but does not specify the emit placement for `decision_census`. The `classify.py` docstring says 'The emit call lands with the first consumer' — it is NOT yet emitted anywhere (grep confirms only a comment reference, no actual `log("decision_census", ...)` call). `binding_walk_census` is emitted in `run_differs.py:535`. The new `decision_census` emit could go in `run_differs.py`, `binding_walk.py`, or `reconcile.py`, and the choice affects which modes and code paths it covers — a structurally ambiguous placement.
findings.location: [CODE] Census key: expand-contract section; AC 'Census emits both keys (expand-contract)'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan that `decision_census` does not currently exist as an emitted event key (the live emit is `binding_walk_census` per `run_differs.py:535`); update the CODE item to state the first step adds the `decision_census` emit as a NEW event (not a migration of an existing key) before the expand-contract sequence begins.
findings.finding: The plan's census-key work is premised on `decision_census` being a canonical key to 'migrate consumers to', implying it is already emitted. But `classify.py:351` only asserts this in a docstring ('Emitted every pass in every mode via SyncLogger.log("decision_census", ...)'); the actual live emit in `run_differs.py:535` uses `"binding_walk_census"`. `decision_census` has never been emitted in practice. The plan's expand-contract framing ('emit BOTH keys, migrate consumers to decision_census, drop binding_walk_census') is structurally correct but rests on a false premise about the current key name — the step of adding the `decision_census` emission is not a 'migration' but a first-ever add. An implementor relying on `classify.py`'s docstring to understand the current state will be misled.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section — 'Census key: expand-contract' item; classify.py:351 vs run_differs.py:535
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Census key: expand-contract' CODE item, prepend: 'NOTE: `decision_census` is not currently emitted — the `classify.py` docstring is aspirational. Step 0 is to add `sync_logger.log("decision_census", **walk.census)` alongside the existing `binding_walk_census` emit in `run_differs.py:535`, then proceed with consumer migration and old-key drop.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the corrupt-baseline detection boundary: either (a) add a `get_baseline_with_status` method to BindingStore that returns `(value, 'absent'|'corrupt'|'ok')`, or (b) document that 'corrupt' here means 'baseline key present but not a dict' and specify that the caller checks `entry.get("baseline") is not None and not isinstance(entry["baseline"], dict)` via a new store predicate — so the fail-closed path is unambiguously implementable.
findings.finding: The plan mandates 'explicit corrupt-store detection' for the consumer swap, noting 'get_baseline cannot itself signal corrupt', but does not specify WHERE this detection lives or WHAT constitutes a corrupt baseline (as opposed to an absent one). In the current `get_baseline` implementation, both 'no baseline key' and 'baseline is not a dict' return `None` identically — the implementer must read the raw binding entry to distinguish them, which requires reaching into BindingStore internals from `outbound_fields.py` or adding a new store method. Without this specification, the corrupt-store fail-closed guarantee is under-specified.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — consumer swap AC: 'corrupt-store fail-closed preserved (explicit detection)'; binding_store.py `get_baseline` lines 299-313

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the minimum Jira project permission set the agent's `JIRA_API_TOKEN` must hold to execute all three heal mutation types (Transition Issues for terminal-transition, Edit Issues for adopt, and the soft-delete/retire path for GC-after-grace), and add a pre-heal permission probe (e.g. a dry-run transition GET against one target issue) that aborts if any required permission is absent.
findings.finding: The OPERATIONAL pre-heal backup and live heal steps assert 'AGENT (ACLI access)' performs mutations (terminal-transition to Done for 5 issues, GC-after-grace deletion for f8b5-2f30→REB-530, adopt for REB-532), but the plan never states what Jira permission scope the agent's credential must hold to execute these specific mutation types. The codebase shows transition uses `POST /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/transitions`, deletion/retire uses binding-store soft-delete plus Jira-side state changes, and the token is read from `JIRA_API_TOKEN`. If the service-account token lacks 'Transition Issues', 'Delete Issues', or 'Edit Issues' permission on the JIRA_PROJECT, the heal will silently fail mid-pass (the applier catches HTTPError and continues) with no abort signal.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL section: 'Capped live heal under bootstrap-strict incl. the GC-after-grace deletion' + AC line '[OPERATIONAL] Capped live heal with a bilateral pre-heal backup'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that explicitly verifies `binding_walk_census` has been removed (e.g., absent from emitted events and from code) after consumers are confirmed migrated to `decision_census`, closing the expand-contract loop.
findings.finding: The expand-contract sequence in the CODE section has three explicit steps — (1) emit both `decision_census` and `binding_walk_census`, (2) migrate consumers to `decision_census`, (3) drop `binding_walk_census` — but no Acceptance Criterion covers the final drop step. The AC only verifies 'Census emits both keys (expand-contract), consumers migrated to `decision_census`', leaving the deletion of the old key as an uncovered deliverable.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — Census key section; AC line: 'Census emits both keys (expand-contract), consumers migrated to `decision_census`'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the census AC: '…; `binding_walk_census` key confirmed absent from emitted events and codebase after migration window (expand-contract closed).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Split this AC into two separate checkboxes: one for census expand-contract key emission + consumer migration, and one for classifier_legacy_divergence alert emission with typed fields (or scoped+documented).
findings.finding: The Census + classifier-divergence AC bundles two distinct deliverables into one checkbox, making it non-atomic and hard to evaluate as a single coherent outcome.
findings.location: AC line '[CODE] Census emits both keys (expand-contract), consumers migrated to decision_census; classifier_legacy_divergence emitted with the typed fields above (or scoped+documented where per-binding legacy decisions aren't extractable)'
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the single AC line with:
- [ ] [CODE] Census emits both `decision_census` AND `binding_walk_census`; all consumers read `decision_census`; `binding_walk_census` drop-listed for next release.
- [ ] [CODE] `classifier_legacy_divergence` alert emitted with typed fields {classifier_count, legacy_count, divergent_pairs, pass_id} (or scoped to extractable code paths with gap documented in docs/jira-sync-setup.md).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific file/section where backup-artifact retention policy will be documented (e.g., add a `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` sub-section or a comment in `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml`) so the 'retention documented' promise has a verifiable target.
findings.finding: The plan states backup artifacts have 'retention documented' but names no target document or section where that retention policy will live, leaving the documentation location unspecified.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — Capped live heal bullet; AC line '[OPERATIONAL] Capped live heal with a bilateral pre-heal backup'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence such as: 'Document retention period (e.g., 90 days) in a `## Artifact Retention` sub-section of `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` and as a `retention-days:` annotation in the workflow YAML.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the required artifact retention period explicitly (e.g. '>=90 days, or org minimum whichever is longer') so the executor has a concrete value to configure and document.
findings.finding: Workflow artifact retention period is unspecified: the plan says 'retention documented' for pre-heal backup artifacts stored in GHA workflow runs, but gives no number; the executor must choose a retention value (GitHub default is 90 days, org policy may differ) with no guidance.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL – Capped live heal paragraph; AC line '[OPERATIONAL] Capped live heal with a bilateral pre-heal backup…; artifacts stored'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the heal operational bullet: 'Retain workflow artifacts for >=90 days (set retention-days: 90 in the workflow step); document this in the workflow YAML comment and in docs/jira-sync-setup.md.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit post-rollback schema-state assertion to the rollback procedure: state that after reverting baseline_consumer_swap to false, bindings.json entries will retain 'baseline'/'baseline_advanced_at' sub-objects (written by the Phase-1 shadow), that the prev_snapshot consumer never reads these fields (get_baseline is not called when the flag is false), and that no manual store cleanup is required — confirming the store is in a known-good intermediate state, not an incorrect one.
findings.finding: The plan's rollback procedure asserts behavioral correctness (reconciler reverts to prev_snapshot arbitration, zero mutations in dry-run) but never asserts the SCHEMA STATE of bindings.json after flag rollback. After flipping baseline_consumer_swap and then rolling it back, bindings.json permanently retains 'baseline'/'baseline_advanced_at' sub-objects on every binding entry (written by the Phase-1 shadow). The T11 rubric explicitly requires asserting the schema state post-rollback, not merely that rollback exits 0 — a rollback can exit 0 while leaving the schema in an incorrect intermediate state.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Flip only after both gates; rollback rehearsed: (a) revert the flag to false and confirm the reconciler reverts to prev_snapshot arbitration with no divergence, AND (b) force one --mode dry-run pass confirming zero mutations'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Remaining OPERATIONAL rollback section and in docs/jira-sync-setup.md, add: 'After rollback, bindings.json entries will contain populated baseline sub-objects from the dual-write shadow; this is the expected schema state. The prev_snapshot consumer does not call get_baseline when baseline_consumer_swap=false, so these fields are inert. No manual store cleanup is needed. Verify with: python -c "import json; d=json.load(open(\'bindings.json\')); assert all(\'baseline\' in e for e in d[\'bindings\'].values()), \'missing baselines\'"'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the access posture of the pre-heal Jira JSON backup artifacts (e.g. 'private repo only; artifact access restricted to maintainers') and, if the repo is or could be public, either exclude PII fields from the export or document a step to delete the artifact after the heal is verified.
findings.finding: The bilateral pre-heal backup ('export affected Jira issue state (JSON via Jira API)') stored as GHA workflow run artifacts has no declared access posture. On a public repo these artifacts are world-readable; on a private repo they are readable by any org member with Actions read access. The Jira JSON export may contain PII (assignee emails, issue descriptions with confidential content). The plan says 'retention documented' but names no access class.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL section: 'PRE-HEAL BACKUP covers BOTH sides: export affected Jira issue state (JSON via Jira API) … Store the artifacts in the workflow run artifacts (retention documented).'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit access declaration to the OPERATIONAL section: 'Artifacts stored as private-repo-only GHA artifacts; if the repo is public, strip PII fields (assignee email, description) from the exported JSON before upload, or delete the artifact immediately after verifying the heal.' Also name a concrete retention period (e.g. 30 days) to fulfill the 'retention documented' claim.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a documentation target for the `classifier_legacy_divergence` alert schema (field types, semantics, and any scoping caveats) — e.g., a sub-section in `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` or an inline docstring reference in the emitting code.
findings.finding: The `classifier_legacy_divergence` alert is a new operational contract (typed schema: classifier_count, legacy_count, divergent_pairs, pass_id) with no documentation target named, making it undiscoverable for operators and future consumers.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting bullet; AC line '[CODE] Census emits both keys … classifier_legacy_divergence emitted with the typed fields above'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a minimum artifact retention period (e.g., 'retain for ≥ 90 days, or until the next full audit cycle') in the plan and confirm it is enforced in the workflow YAML, not left to undocumented repo defaults.
findings.finding: The pre-heal backup artifact retention period is described only as 'retention documented' without specifying a minimum retention window. If GHA purges artifacts before a rollback is needed (e.g., default 90 days may be shortened by repo settings), the bilateral backup intended to make the heal recoverable becomes unavailable.
findings.location: [OPERATIONAL] Capped live heal — 'Store the artifacts in the workflow run artifacts (retention documented)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `retention-days: 90` (or appropriate value) to the `upload-artifact` step in `reconcile-bridge.yml` and state the chosen period explicitly in the AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate reconcile_check.py's classify() call-site (reconcile_check.py:372) as a consumer in the classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting scope section, and explicitly state whether it is in or out of the alert's coverage.
findings.finding: reconcile_check.py is also an unenumerated outside-dir consumer of classify.py: it loads classify via _load_classify() (reconcile_check.py:50-58) and calls classify() at line 372 with entry.get('baseline') as the baseline argument. The plan's classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting modifies classify.py's decision surface, but reconcile_check.py's classify() call-site is not enumerated as an affected consumer.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting; src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile_check.py:50-58, 357-372

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence rationale in the plan for why the census expand-contract and classifier divergence alerting belong in this ticket (e.g., 'required observability before the live flip can be safely validated') or explicitly defer them to a named sibling ticket with a cross-reference.
findings.finding: The plan places two substantial, independently-motivated [CODE] items — the census key expand-contract migration and the classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting — in scope without stating any dependency relationship to the Phase-3 consumer swap, shadow-pass validation, or live heal described in the title. This creates ambiguity about whether they are prerequisites, co-deliverables, or scope drift; and risks the agent treating them as optional or, conversely, blocking the flip on unrelated work.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section — 'Census key: expand-contract' and 'classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting' items; AC line 'Census emits both keys … classifier_legacy_divergence emitted'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider extracting the Census expand-contract migration (`decision_census` / `binding_walk_census` dual-emit + consumer migration) into its own ticket, since it has no dependency on the consumer swap flag, the shadow-pass gate, or the live heal and can be independently released.
findings.finding: The Census expand-contract migration (emit both `decision_census` and `binding_walk_census`, migrate consumers, drop old key) is independently valuable and releasable from the consumer swap rollout — it has a different reason to change (API consumer migration / schema evolution), no dependency on the `baseline_consumer_swap` flag, the shadow-pass streak, or the live heal, and its AC is satisfiable without any operational steps. Bundling it into this ticket introduces a second independent concern.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — [CODE] Census key: expand-contract section; AC line 'Census emits both keys (expand-contract), consumers migrated to `decision_census`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge and justify the new `outbound_fields` → `BindingStore` cross-component coupling in the plan (or mitigate it by injecting the baseline value as a parameter to `_local_matches_prev` rather than having the function reach into the store directly).
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new cross-component dependency — `outbound_fields._local_matches_prev` (and its `outbound_differ` caller) now read directly from `BindingStore.get_baseline` — but provides no interface boundary or explicit justification for why this coupling is acceptable rather than, e.g., passing the baseline value as a parameter.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — Phase-3 consumer swap bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Prefer passing the resolved baseline value as a parameter: `_local_matches_prev(local, prev, baseline=None)` where the caller (e.g., `outbound_differ`) resolves `BindingStore.get_baseline` and passes the result. This keeps `outbound_fields` free of a direct store dependency and makes the None->local-wins mapping testable without a store.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the architectural decision (why BindingStore.get_baseline replaces prev_snapshot as the arbitration source, alternatives considered, and the None/corrupt-store behavioral contract) in an ADR or a `## Design Rationale` section in `docs/jira-sync-setup.md`, not just the operational flip procedure.
findings.finding: The architectural decision to replace `prev_snapshot` with `BindingStore.get_baseline` as the canonical arbitration source for the outbound consumer is a novel, irreversible design choice with significant behavioral implications, yet no ADR or design-doc update is planned — only a flag/flip procedure note in `docs/jira-sync-setup.md`.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — Phase-3 consumer swap bullet; AC line '[CODE] `reconciler.baseline_consumer_swap` flag added + documented'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concurrency-safety contract for BindingStore (e.g., file-level lock, atomic rename, or workflow-level concurrency: cancel-in-progress) so that concurrent `reconcile-bridge.yml` runs cannot produce torn reads or writes between `baseline_dual_write` and `get_baseline`.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no atomicity or ownership discipline for concurrent access to the shared BindingStore: baseline_dual_write (Phase-1 SHADOW) writes to it while get_baseline reads from it, and the `reconcile-bridge.yml` workflow fires every 20 minutes with no overlap-prevention guard. Two runs that start within the same 20-min window could interleave a write and a read on the same binding entry, producing a torn read in `_local_matches_prev` or a corrupt-store false-positive.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — `baseline_dual_write`/`get_baseline` path; `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml` cadence note
findings.suggested_fix: Add `concurrency: group: reconcile-bridge / cancel-in-progress: true` (or equivalent serialisation) to `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml`, and document in the plan that BindingStore mutations are atomic (e.g., write-to-temp then rename). This prevents overlapping runs and closes the TOCTOU between the dual-write and the consumer-swap read.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate reconcile_check.py as an outside-dir consumer of outbound_fields.py and confirm its _compare_pair path is unaffected by (or correctly updated for) the _local_matches_prev consumer swap.
findings.finding: reconcile_check.py is an unenumerated outside-dir consumer of outbound_fields.py: it loads the module via _load_outbound_fields() (reconcile_check.py:70) and calls outbound_fields helpers (_extract_jira_field, _assignee_matches) for live field comparison. The plan's consumer swap changes the behavior of _local_matches_prev in outbound_fields.py under the new flag, but does not mention reconcile_check.py as an affected caller that needs review.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section — Phase-3 consumer swap; src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile_check.py:61-70
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note in the CODE section: 'reconcile_check.py loads outbound_fields for field normalization but does NOT call _local_matches_prev; confirm this remains true and add a test guard if the function's call surface changes under the flag.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale for log-derived streak (e.g. 'log-derivation avoids adding a new write surface to the binding store and is auditable from existing sync-log infrastructure') so implementers understand the design intent.
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for choosing a log-derived streak (querying sync-log history for `baseline_shadow_check` events) over a persistent counter in the binding store or a dedicated state file. A persistent counter would be equally restart-safe and simpler to query. The absence of a stated positive rationale leaves implementers uncertain whether the log-derivation approach is intentional design or an oversight.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL section — '>=10 CONSECUTIVE clean shadow passes ... Streak is DERIVED from the sync-log history'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
